title: Protocol: clinical trial neglect analysis of 15 Africa-burden diseases
primary_estimand: Neglect Index (Africa burden % / Africa trial share %) for each disease
body: This cross-sectional registry study will quantify the clinical trial deficit for 15 diseases that disproportionately affect Africa. We will search ClinicalTrials.gov for interventional trials in each condition across African countries and globally, extracting trial counts, sponsors, phases, and status. The 15 index diseases are Burkitt lymphoma, Kaposi sarcoma, trypanosomiasis, schistosomiasis, onchocerciasis, trachoma, lymphatic filariasis, leishmaniasis, rabies, podoconiosis, mycetoma, noma, rheumatic heart disease, snakebite envenoming, and Buruli ulcer. The primary outcome is the Neglect Index for each disease, calculated as Africa burden share divided by Africa trial share. Secondary outcomes include identification of absolute-zero diseases, sponsor classification, and comparison with orphan drug trial investment in high-income countries. Disease burden estimates will be drawn from WHO and Global Burden of Disease sources. The study is limited to ClinicalTrials.gov and cannot detect trials registered only in national or regional platforms.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.value: Neglect Index = burden share / trial share for each disease
Zero-trial count, sponsor classification, aggregate counts, HIC comparison
All 15 diseases enumerated by name in protocol
17 African countries + 'Africa', per-disease queries, API v2
WHO and GBD, with uncertainty noted
No patient-level data, public registry only
